hypothesis: Systems-optimizer replayable proofs become safer if every command list must stay shell-safe and single-command, so runner evidence cannot hide chained shell steps behind an otherwise valid CLAW prefix.
measurement: Before the ratchet, a synchronized falsification that changed `sysopt.runner-stability-evidence-boundary-lock.evidence_commands[0]` to `node CLAW/scripts/recover-cycle.mjs --max-age-minutes=0 && echo leaked-proof` in both backlog and hypothesis-card surfaces still verified clean, so replayable runner proof could be widened with chained shell work behind a valid CLAW prefix. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects shell control operators and multi-command chaining across systems-optimizer command lists while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if shell-chained systems-optimizer command strings fail verification and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer command lists must stay shell-safe single commands; root-relative prefixes alone do not prevent chained shell steps from widening replayable proof.
